meta.toolResultPreview: 1: # pi-orchestrator 2: 3: Type `/orchestrate` in any repo. meta.toolResultPreview: 1: # Architecture 2: 3: ## Overview 4: 5: pi-orchestrator turns `/orchestrate` into a structured, multi-agent workflow. It scans the codebase, proposes improvements, plans the work, executes steps in git worktrees when parallelism is possible, reviews the results, and iterates until the user is satisfied. 6: 7: Based on the [Agentic Coding Flywheel](https://agent-flywheel.com/). 8: 9: ## Architecture Diagram 10: 11: ``` 12: /orchestrate 13: │ 14: ├─► orch_profile — Scan repo + load compound memory from prior runs 15: │ └─ Discovery mode: 📋 Standard or 🚀 Creative (broader ideation, return 7) 16: │ 17: ├─► orch_discover — LLM generates 3–7 ideas (minimum enforced) 18: │ 19: ├─► orch_select —…

vision.rationale: README.md provides a clear project vision: a one-command orchestration system for AI-assisted code improvement with multi-model planning, parallel execution, multi-agent review, and compound memory. docs/architecture.md expands on this with the Agentic Coding Flywheel methodology and the full workflow diagram.

architecture.rationale: docs/architecture.md is a comprehensive architecture document covering all phases (discovery, planning, implementation, review, completion), the scan contract abstraction, context priority order, parallel execution with worktrees, SwarmTender monitoring, Sophia integration, and the project structure. docs/setup.md provides configuration architecture. The source files in src/ implement the documented architecture.

principles_guidelines.rationale: Principles are embedded across multiple sources: (1) docs/architecture.md defines the context priority order, flywheel-derived prompt patterns, and dependency analysis conventions; (2) src/prompts.ts encodes agent behavior principles via purpose-built prompt templates; (3) SOPHIA.yaml defines contract requirements, merge policies, and trust model; (4) The scan contract in docs/architecture.md establishes the provider abstraction principle. No standalone CONTRIBUTING.md or coding style guide exists, but the project's conventions are well-documented in the architecture docs.
